name: Chemistry Reasoning Data Augmentation Strategy Comparison
population_or_context: Undergraduate chemistry students' reasoning about reaction mechanisms.
sample_size: Not extracted in this baseline pass; source abstract describes multiple augmentation strategies and validation conditions.
methods: Compared collecting additional student data, chatbot paraphrasing of existing responses, and chatbot generation of synthetic responses for machine-learning scoring.
education_context: Postsecondary chemistry formative assessment focused on causal reasoning about organic reaction mechanisms.
response_origin: Hybrid: authentic student responses, chatbot-paraphrased responses, and chatbot-generated synthetic responses.
rubric_label_space: Categorization of levels of causality in students' chemistry reasoning; the paper reports expanding to a more detailed scoring rubric.
label_source: Human-coded or research-coded chemistry reasoning categories are implied by the scoring-rubric and validation setup; detailed label source requires full-text follow-up.
generation_model: Chatbots or generative AI models; exact model details not extracted in this baseline pass.
generation_method: Paraphrasing existing student responses and prompting chatbots to generate synthetic responses.
prompt_strategy: Not extracted in this baseline pass.
evaluation_method: Compared machine-human score agreement across validation conditions and augmentation strategies.
scorer_use: Training augmentation, scorer performance improvement, and scoring-rubric refinement.
real_response_comparator: Yes. The study compared augmentation against additional authentic student data and validation conditions with human scores.
sparsity_or_imbalance_motivation: The study frames diverse training data as hard to gather because capturing nuanced student reasoning is resource-constrained.
synthetic_data_research_role: Synthetic and paraphrased responses expanded training-data size and heterogeneity and supported a more fine-grained rubric.
reported_metrics: Best machine-human score agreements were reported when authentic and/or paraphrased responses were combined with chatbot responses.
limitations: The extraction relies on the open abstract. A full-text follow-up on 2026-06-03 found only abstract/metadata access from this workspace; ScienceDirect returned CAPTCHA/IP-block pages for browser/PDF paths and metadata APIs exposed no article body or PDF. Full methods should be reviewed before making model-specific claims.
